office_does: One of two House members for LD 48 (Redmond, Bellevue, Kirkland area); serves a two-year term voting on state budgets, taxes, and all state law.
race_blurb: Both candidates advance to the general regardless of the primary result. An appointed Democratic incumbent with broad establishment endorsements faces a Republican retired CPA running an almost entirely fiscal platform.

candidates.summary: Appointed to the House in January 2025 after serving on the Redmond City Council; Redmond native with a research and community-engagement background.
highlights: Says he secured childcare and healthcare investments and 'passed more laws than any legislator this year,' citing bills on affordable housing, gun-violence prevention, and immigrant protections [S1]
Former Redmond city councilmember and UW student body president; appointed to succeed Vandana Slatter [S1][S3]
Endorsed by Rep. DelBene, Gov. Ferguson, the WA State Labor Council, and teachers and nurses unions [S1][S2]

experience.basis: Appointed incumbent with about 1.5 years of legislative record plus prior city council service — a record case, but a short one.

spending.basis: Campaigns on secured investments in childcare, healthcare, housing, and education.

housing.basis: Cites bills on affordable housing (candidate's own characterization); public-investment framing.

immigration.basis: Cites passed bills on immigrant protections (candidate's own characterization).

climate.basis: Lists climate action among priorities; endorsed by Washington Conservation Action.

social.basis: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance; no direct statements in the dossier.

candidates.summary: Retired CPA and financial systems consultant with 40 years of auditing experience, making his first run for state office on a fiscal-accountability platform.
highlights: Pledges to reject new taxes: Washington 'has a spending problem, not an income problem' [S1][S2]
Pledges to spearhead fraud audits and make state spending and performance metrics publicly accessible [S1][S2]
Accepts only individual contributions — no PAC or union money [S1][S2]

experience.basis: First-time state candidate with no prior elected public office; runs on an outside auditor's profile.

taxes.basis: Pledges to reject new taxes, framing the state as having 'a spending problem, not an income problem.'

spending.basis: Platform centers fraud audits, efficiency, and public spending/performance transparency.

reform.basis: Pledges to accept only individual contributions — no PAC or union money.
